originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: Big News in Cameroon: A New Helper for President Biya!
content:
Hello, my little friends from all over Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I have some exciting news to share with you today. We're going to travel with our minds to a wonderful country called Cameroon! It's in Central Africa, and it's full of beautiful nature and friendly people. We have a very interesting story coming from there. It's about a very important leader and a new helper for him. Let's find out more together! This news is quite special for the country.

Meet President Paul Biya!

In Cameroon, there is a very important person named President Paul Biya. He is the leader of the whole country. Think of him like the captain of a big ship, or the head teacher of a very, very big school! His job is to help everyone in Cameroon and make good rules. President Biya is 93 years old. That's very, very old, almost as old as your great-grandparents might be! Imagine all the stories he could tell.

What is a President?

A President is someone who leads a country. They help make important choices for all the people living there. They make sure schools are built, hospitals are running, and everyone is safe. It's a very big job with lots of responsibilities. They have to think about roads, water, food, and how people can live happily. President Biya has been doing this job for a very, very long time.

What is a Deputy?

Now, the big news is that President Biya is getting a 'deputy.' What is a deputy? A deputy is like a helper or an assistant. Imagine you are building a big Lego castle. If you have a deputy, that person helps you find the right pieces and put them together. They help you with your big job. For a president, a deputy helps with the many tasks of leading a country. They share some of the work.

Why is This News Special?

This news is super special because President Biya has not had a main deputy for 43 years! Yes, you heard that right, 43 years! That's longer than your parents have been alive. Imagine doing a big job all by yourself for so long. Now, he will have someone important right by his side to help. This is the very first time in more than four decades that such a position will be filled. It's a big change for Cameroon.

A Long Time in Charge!

President Biya has been the leader of Cameroon for a really, really long time. He started being the President when many of your parents were just little babies, or even before they were born! He has seen many, many changes in Cameroon. Last year, people in Cameroon voted again, and he was chosen to be President for another term. This shows that many people in his country trust him to lead.

Did You Know? About Cameroon

Did you know that Cameroon is sometimes called "Africa in miniature"? This is because it has almost all the different types of land you can find in Africa! It has beautiful beaches, big mountains, green rainforests, and even dry savannas. It's like a small version of the whole continent! Its capital city is called Yaoundé. Many different languages are spoken there, including French and English.

Why is a Helper Good?

Having a helper, or a deputy, is a very good thing. Think about when you play with your friends. If you have a big project, like cleaning up your room or building a sandcastle, it's much easier with help, right? A deputy can help the President make decisions, meet people, and work on different projects. It means the work can get done faster and better. It helps to share the load.

What Happens Next?

Now that President Biya will have a deputy, this person will help him with his important work. The deputy will learn a lot from President Biya. They will work together to make Cameroon a great place for everyone. It means there will be two very important people working closely for the country. This can bring new ideas and new energy to the government.

Did You Know? About Leaders

Did you know that leaders like presidents have many important meetings? They talk to other leaders from different countries. They also listen to the people in their own country. Being a leader means listening carefully and trying to make good choices for everyone. It's a big responsibility, and sometimes it's good to have someone to talk to and share ideas with. That's what a deputy can do.

A Big Step for Cameroon!

This is a big step for Cameroon. It shows that even very experienced leaders like President Biya appreciate having help. It means more people are working together to build a strong future for the children of Cameroon. It is a new chapter in the country's story. We hope that President Biya and his new deputy will work very well together to help the country grow and shine! It's exciting news for all.
summary: Cameroon's very old leader, President Paul Biya, will get a helper called a deputy for the first time in 43 years, which is big news for the country.
whyMatters: This matters because having a helper means the President can share his big job, and it can bring new ideas and energy to make Cameroon even better for everyone.

originalTitle: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
title: Senegal's Leaders Take a Break from Flying to Save Money!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all across Africa! I'm your super kid-friendly news reporter, and I have some interesting news to share with you today from a wonderful country called Senegal!

Hello from Senegal!

Senegal is a beautiful country in West Africa. It's known for its sunny beaches, delicious food, and friendly people. Imagine a place where you can see colourful fishing boats and hear lively music! The capital city is Dakar, and it's a very busy and exciting place.

Important Helpers in Senegal

Every country has special helpers who work very hard to make sure everything runs smoothly. We call these important helpers "ministers." Think of them like the super-responsible big brothers and sisters who help manage your home, but for a whole country! Ministers help with things like making sure schools are good, hospitals have what they need, and roads are safe. They travel often to meet other leaders and learn new things to help their country grow.

A Special New Rule: No Flying for Now!

Now, here's the big news! The important helpers in Senegal have a new rule. For a little while, they won't be flying to other countries for their work trips. This means they will be staying right there in Senegal. It’s like when your parents decide to have a fun stay-at-home weekend instead of going on a long trip. They are doing this for a very important reason: to save money!

Did You Know?

Senegal is famous for its "Teranga" which means hospitality in their local language, Wolof. It means they are very welcoming to visitors!

What is Fuel? And Why Does it Cost So Much?

You know how your bicycle needs your legs to move, or your toy car needs batteries? Well, big vehicles like cars, buses, and airplanes need something called "fuel" to move! Fuel is like the food for engines. It helps them go "vroom vroom" and fly high in the sky! Most fuel comes from something called "oil." Oil is a thick, black liquid found deep, deep underground. It takes a lot of work to find it and bring it up to the surface. Then, it goes to special factories where it's changed into things like petrol for cars, diesel for buses, and jet fuel for airplanes.

Did You Know?

Oil was formed from tiny plants and animals that lived millions of years ago! They got buried under layers of rock and dirt, and over time, turned into oil. It's like super ancient juice!

The Price of Fuel is Going Up!

Lately, the cost of this fuel has become very, very high. Imagine your favourite sweet treat. If suddenly everyone in the world wanted that exact sweet treat, and there weren't many left, the shop might have to make it more expensive, right? That's a bit like what's happening with oil and fuel. Things happening around the world can make oil more expensive, which means fuel becomes more expensive too. The government in Senegal had a plan for how much money they would spend on fuel this year. This plan is called a "budget." But now, because the price of fuel has gone so high, it costs almost *twice* as much as they thought! Imagine if you had money to buy one toy, but then the toy suddenly cost two times more – you wouldn't have enough money anymore, would you?

Why is This a Big Deal for Senegal?

When the price of fuel goes up so much, it means the government has to spend a lot more of its money just to buy fuel. This is like having less pocket money because your favourite snack now costs way more. If they spend too much money on fuel, they might have less money for other really important things. What other important things? Well, things like: * Building new schools so more kids can learn. * Making sure hospitals have enough medicine and doctors. * Fixing roads so cars and buses can travel safely. * Helping farmers grow more food. So, by telling their important helpers not to fly to other countries for a while, the government is trying to save money. When ministers travel, they use airplanes which need lots of expensive jet fuel. They also use cars when they get to other countries. All this travel costs a lot of money!

Did You Know?

A big airplane can use as much fuel as 50 cars in just one hour! That's a lot of food for the engine!

Saving Money to Help Everyone

By saving money on these trips, Senegal's government hopes to have more money left over for those other important things like schools and hospitals. It's a clever way to be careful with money, especially when things are getting more expensive. It shows that they are thinking about what's best for all the people in Senegal.

What Can We Learn?

This news from Senegal reminds us that everything costs money, and it’s good to be smart about how we spend it. When things get expensive, we sometimes need to make clever choices to save. We can all help save energy too! * Turn off lights when you leave a room. * Walk or cycle instead of asking for a car ride if it's safe. * Don't leave the TV or computer on when no one is using it. Every little bit helps! It’s like saving your own pocket money for something special. When a whole country saves, it can make a big difference! Isn't it amazing how a simple choice like "no flying" can help a whole country? Keep being curious, my friends, and learn more about our amazing continent, Africa! See you next time!
summary: The country of Senegal has asked its important helpers, called ministers, to stop flying to other countries for work to save money because the fuel for cars and planes has become very expensive.
whyMatters: This is important because when the government saves money on fuel, they can use it for other important things like building schools or helping hospitals, which helps everyone in the country.

originalTitle: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
title: Sad Story from Nigeria: Promises Not Kept for Abuja's First Families
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, super friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, coming to you live from wonderful Africa! Today, we have a very important story from a big country called Nigeria. It's about a special city and some people who feel a little sad. Let's learn together!

Nigeria's Special City: Abuja

Did you know Nigeria is one of the biggest countries in Africa? It has so many people and amazing places! Long ago, Nigeria's leaders decided they needed a new, special capital city. A capital city is like the main office for a whole country. It's where the President works and where big decisions are made. They wanted a city that everyone could feel was fair and in the middle of the country. So, they picked a place and called it Abuja! Did you know? Abuja is not on the coast like some big cities. It's more in the middle of Nigeria!

Where Did Abuja Come From?

Imagine you want to build a brand-new house. You need an empty piece of land, right? Well, building a whole city like Abuja needed a LOT of land. Before Abuja was built, many families already lived on that land. They had their homes, their farms, and their schools. Their great-grandparents probably lived there too! It was their special place. Think about it like this: if someone wanted to build a big new playground where your house is, how would you feel?

Moving Day for Many Families

When the government decided to build Abuja, they told these families they had to move. It must have been a very big change for them. Imagine packing up everything you own! It's like moving to a new school or a new house, but on a much bigger scale. Many, many families had to leave their old homes. It's a bit like when little ducklings have to follow their mama duck to a new pond. It can be exciting, but also a little bit scary or sad to leave what you know.

What Was Promised?

Now, here's the important part. When the government asked these families to move, they made some big promises. A promise is when you say you will definitely do something. For example, your friend might promise to share their toys. The government promised these families that they would help them. They said, "Don't worry! We will give you new homes. We will give you land for new farms. We will build new schools for your children. We will help you start fresh in a new place." These were very important promises to make sure the families would be okay after moving. Did you know? Keeping promises makes people trust you. It's like playing a game and sticking to the rules!

But What Happened?

Sadly, our news today tells us something difficult. Many years have passed since these promises were made. The people who moved for Abuja are saying that the government did not keep all their promises. They say they didn't get new homes. They didn't get new farms. Their children didn't always get new schools. This makes them feel very, very sad and even a little bit angry. They feel like they were forgotten after they helped build a big, beautiful city for Nigeria. Imagine if your friend promised to share their toys, but then they didn't. You would feel upset, right?

Life Without Promises

Think about what happens if you don't have a home. Where do you sleep? What if you don't have a farm anymore? How do you grow food to eat? What if there are no schools for the children? They cannot learn to read and write, which is so important for their future. These families are finding it very hard to live because the help they were promised never came. They helped build a great city, but they feel they were left behind. This is a big problem because everyone deserves a safe home and a chance to learn and grow. Did you know? Every child deserves to go to school and learn amazing things!

Why Do People Want a Voice?

The people say, "We want a voice in our land!" What does that mean? It means they want to be heard. They want to talk to the leaders. They want to remind everyone about the promises that were made so long ago. It's like when you want to tell your parents something important. You want them to listen to you, right? These families want the government to listen to their problems and help them now. They want the government to remember them and fulfill those old promises. They want fairness.

What Can We Learn?

This story from Nigeria teaches us something super important. It teaches us about promises. It teaches us about being fair to everyone. And it teaches us that when someone moves or makes a big sacrifice, we must always help them. It's important that people who are in charge, like our leaders, always remember everyone. Especially the people who helped make big things happen. We should always try to make sure everyone is treated fairly and has a safe place to live.

Let's Hope for Fairness!

So, our hope today is that the leaders in Nigeria will listen to these families. We hope they will remember the promises they made. And we hope they can find a way to help these people so they can have happy homes and a good future. Every person in Africa, and everywhere in the world, deserves to be treated with kindness and fairness. That's all from your friendly news reporter for today! Keep being kind and remember to always keep your promises! Bye-bye for now!
summary: Many years ago, families moved so Nigeria could build its capital city, Abuja, but they say the government did not keep its promises to give them new homes and help.
whyMatters: It is important for governments to keep their promises to people and treat everyone fairly, especially when they ask people to make big changes for a whole country.

originalTitle: Trump offers no concrete plan as Strait of Hormuz closure shocks Europe
title: Trump offers no concrete plan as Strait of Hormuz closure shocks Europe
content: Economist James Meadway says US President Donald Trump offers threats but no clear plan to end the crisis.

originalTitle: Iran’s ex-FM Zarif proposes peace roadmap; Gulf points at erosion of trust
title: A Big Plan for Peace: Helping Countries Be Friends!
content:
Hello, Little News Explorers!

Hello, hello, my super curious friends all across Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter here, ready to share a really interesting story with you today. We’re going to talk about a big idea that could help countries far away become better friends. Isn't that exciting? Imagine you are playing with your friends, and sometimes, maybe you have a little disagreement about a toy or a game. What do you do? You talk about it, right? You try to find a way to play nicely again and be happy together. Well, sometimes countries, which are like very big families, have big disagreements too.

A Big Plan for Peace!

Far, far away, in a country called Iran, there is a very important person. His name is Mr. Zarif. He used to be a super big helper for his country, like a wise elder who talks to other families to solve problems. He had a super clever idea to help everyone get along better. He called it a "peace roadmap." Did you know? A roadmap is like a map you use to find your way when you go on a trip. A "peace roadmap" means a plan, step by step, to find a way to peace and friendship between countries!

Who is Mr. Zarif?

Mr. Zarif was once the "Foreign Minister" of Iran. That's a big, important job! It means he was the main person who talked to other countries for Iran. He helped Iran make friends and solve problems with people from all over the world. Think of it like this: if your school wants to play a game with another school, the head boy or head girl might talk to the other school's leaders. Mr. Zarif did that, but for a whole country! He tried to find ways for Iran to be safe and happy.

What is a "Peace Roadmap"?

So, what exactly is this "peace roadmap" idea? Mr. Zarif's plan is like a special deal. He said, "If we do some things, then other countries can do some things too, and everyone will be happier!" It's a bit like when you share your toys with a friend, and then your friend shares their sweets with you. Everyone wins! Mr. Zarif wants to help countries win together.

What About "Nuclear Power"?

One big part of Mr. Zarif's plan is about "nuclear power." Now, this sounds like a very grown-up word, doesn't it? Let me explain it simply. Nuclear power is a special kind of energy. It can be used for good things, like making electricity to light up our homes, run our TVs, and charge our phones! It's like a very, very strong battery. But sometimes, if countries make too much of this special power, other countries worry that it could be used to make very powerful bombs. These are not good for anyone, as they can cause a lot of harm. So, countries want to be very, very careful. Did you know? Nuclear power can also be used in hospitals to help doctors see inside our bodies and find out what's wrong! It’s not all about bombs.

Why Do Countries Worry?

Mr. Zarif’s plan suggests putting "limits" on Iran’s nuclear programme. What does "limits" mean? It means saying, "Okay, Iran will only make a little bit of this special power, just enough for good things like electricity, and not too much that other countries would worry about bombs." It's like when your parents tell you, "You can play video games for one hour, but not all day." Those are limits, right? They help keep things fair and safe. So, these limits are to help other countries feel safe and trust Iran.

The Busy Sea Road: Strait of Hormuz

Another part of the plan is about a very special place called the "Strait of Hormuz." This is not a road on land, but a very narrow water path in the sea. Imagine a super busy highway, but for ships! Many, many big ships travel through this narrow sea path every single day. They carry important things like oil, which helps cars run and homes stay warm. It's like a major road for everything that travels by sea in that part of the world. Did you know? The Strait of Hormuz is so important because it connects many countries that need to sell their oil to other parts of the world. If ships can't pass easily, it causes big problems for everyone!

Why is the Strait Important?

Mr. Zarif said that in his plan, the Strait of Hormuz should be "reopened." This means making sure that this important sea road is always safe and open for all ships to pass through easily, without any problems or worries. If ships can't go through, countries can't get the things they need, and they can't sell their own goods. It’s like if the only road to your market was suddenly closed – it would be very difficult for everyone! Keeping the strait open helps trade and friendship.

What are "Sanctions"?

Now, for what Iran would get in return. Mr. Zarif's plan says that if Iran does these things – limits its nuclear power and keeps the Strait of Hormuz open – then other countries should "end sanctions." What are "sanctions"? This is when many countries are unhappy with another country, so they say, "We won't buy your things, and we won't sell you our things." It makes it very hard for that country to get money or important items they need, like medicines or new machines. It's a bit like if your friends decide not to play with you for a while because they are upset. It makes it hard to get things done or have fun. Sanctions are meant to make a country change its ways.

Making Friends Again!

So, "end to sanctions" means removing those rules. It means other countries would say, "Okay, we believe you now. We will start buying your things and selling you ours again." This would help Iran's people have a better life, get the things they need, and make more money. It’s about building trust again, like when you and your friend make up after a fight and start playing happily together again!

What Did Other Countries Say?

But not everyone was jumping for joy right away. The countries around Iran, which are called "Gulf countries," heard about Mr. Zarif's plan. They said that it's a good idea to talk about peace, but they also felt something important was missing. They talked about something called "erosion of trust."

Why "Trust" is Important

"Erosion of trust" means that countries or people have stopped believing in each other. It's like if someone keeps making promises but doesn't always keep them. After a while, it becomes hard to trust that person again. The Gulf countries felt that there had been too many problems in the past, and it was hard for them to trust Iran completely right now, even with a new plan. They said that true peace needs strong trust, like strong glue that holds friendships together. Did you know? Trust is super important for friendships and for countries. When there is trust, everyone feels safer and happier!

Learning to Be Friends

So, this story is about big grown-ups trying to solve big problems between countries. It's a bit like when you and your friends learn to share, take turns, and say sorry. It takes time and effort to build trust and make peace. Mr. Zarif’s plan is one idea, and now all the countries need to talk and decide how they can move forward together, making sure everyone feels safe and respected.

Our African Friends

Even though this story is happening far away from our beautiful continent of Africa, it's important for us to know about it. When countries are peaceful, it helps the whole world. When there is peace, people can build better schools, grow more food, and help everyone live happier lives. We always hope for peace everywhere, just like we hope for peace in our own homes and communities here in Africa.

What Happens Next?

Now, countries will keep talking. They will think about Mr. Zarif's plan and what the Gulf countries said. They will try to find a way to make sure everyone feels safe, trusted, and can live in peace. It's a big puzzle to solve, but with kindness and talking, anything is possible! Keep being curious, my friends!
summary: An important person from Iran, Mr. Zarif, has shared a special plan to help countries be friends by limiting special power and opening a sea path, hoping to stop old disagreements and end difficult rules called sanctions.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries are peaceful and trust each other, everyone in the world is safer and happier, and we can all live better lives without fighting.
